vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle has a CVT — shift lock slot location: Cap near selector base on console.
Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
CVT transmissions: do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with driven wheels on ground — flatbed strongly preferred.
vehicles.notes: Manual: clutch to N. AWD available on Mazda3 (2019+).
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near selector base on console.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near selector base on console.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base (older) or electronic selector slot (2024+)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Cap near selector base (older) or electronic selector slot (2024+).
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. CX-90 PHEV: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. CX-90 PHEV: 12V only.

shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near selector base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near selector base.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near selector base (auto only).
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood (driver side on ND gen), or passenger side on NC gen.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near selector base (auto only).
vehicles.notes: Manual: clutch to N. Very low clearance.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed — low clearance, flatbed preferred. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

knownIssues.title: Very Low Ground Clearance
knownIssues.detail: Ground clearance on the Miata is approximately 4.5 inches. Use shallow approach ramps for flatbed loading. The front air dam and soft front lip are easily damaged by standard ramp angles.

knownIssues.title: Soft Top Convertible Damage Risk
knownIssues.detail: Soft top models are easily damaged if straps or chains contact the roof. Use hood straps or wheel lift methods. Never run straps over the soft top.

battery.location: Under hood (driver side on ND gen), or passenger side on NC gen
battery.warnings: Very low clearance. Soft top: never run straps over the roof.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on center console (auto only)
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
AUTOMATIC: Locate the shift lock cap near selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead. Insert key and press firmly.
While holding, press brake. Move selector to Neutral.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION (most common): depress clutch fully, move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Manual trans: depress clutch, select Neutral.
Automatic: shift lock release as above.
vehicles.notes: Manual transmission is the most common variant. Lightweight sports car — high value. Flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear. Flatbed strongly preferred to avoid body damage.
towNotes: RWD: lift from rear.
Lightweight body — very easy to scratch. Flatbed preferred.
Manual trans: depress clutch, select Neutral — no shift lock.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral.
IMPORTANT: The Renesis rotary engine must be warm before restarting — a cold rotary tow call is common after flooding. Do not attempt a restart without warming procedure.
vehicles.notes: Renesis rotary engine. Very common flood/no-start tow. Battery under hood. Manual: depress clutch.
vehicles.warn: Rotary engine: very prone to flooding (over-choking on cold starts). Extremely common tow call.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear. Flatbed preferred.
towNotes: RWD: lift from rear.
Rotary engine: very common flood tow — do not attempt restart without proper procedure.

knownIssues.title: Rotary Engine Flooding — Extremely Common Tow Call
knownIssues.detail: The RX-8 Renesis rotary engine is notorious for flooding (fuel-wash of apex seals) when started cold, driven short distances, or improperly shut down. This is the most common reason the RX-8 is towed. Do not attempt to 'jump start and go' — the engine requires a specific flooding recovery procedure. Tow to a shop.

battery.warnings: Rotary engine frequently floods — this is a shop repair, not a roadside fix.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small cap near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
Transfer case: shift to 2WD before road towing.
vehicles.notes: Ford Escape platform. 4WD: must shift to 2WD before road towing. Manual: depress clutch, select Neutral.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front. 4WD: shift TC to 2WD first, then lift rear. Flatbed preferred.

positions: 4WD Auto — NOT safe for road towing
4WD Hi — NOT safe for dry road
steps: IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first, shift to 2WD, then disconnect.

towNotes: Ford Escape platform. TC must be in 2WD for road towing.
DEAD BATTERY: Jump first, shift to 2WD, THEN disconnect.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front. AWD: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.
AWD: flatbed preferred.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on floor console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small cap near gear selector base on floor console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, shift to Neutral.
Transfer case: shift to 2WD before road towing. Manual TC floor lever on most.
vehicles.notes: Ford Ranger-based. Manual TC floor lever. Manual: depress clutch, select Neutral.
vehicles.warn: Shift TC to 2H before road towing.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear. 4WD: shift TC to 2H first, then lift rear.

positions: 4WD Hi — NOT safe for dry road
steps: MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch, select Neutral.

towNotes: Ford Ranger platform. Manual TC floor lever — must be in 2WD before road towing.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch, select Neutral.
